title: Dashboard Screen with Policy Summary and Quick Actions
body:
## Summary
Build the Dashboard screen displaying policy summary cards, coverage details, and quick action buttons for File a Claim and Download Proof of Insurance.

## Current Behaviour
No dashboard exists for authenticated policyholders.

## Proposed Solution
Implement React dashboard page matching the Dashboard mockup, fetching policy data from backend and rendering policy cards with quick action buttons.

## Scope
- Create /dashboard route and Dashboard component in client/src/pages/
- Fetch policy summary from GET /api/policies/:policyNumber endpoint
- Display policy number, coverage type, limits, and deductibles in card layout
- Add File a Claim button routing to /file-claim
- Add Download Proof of Insurance button triggering PDF download

## Implementation
- Create Dashboard.jsx component with useEffect to fetch policy data on mount
- Call axios.get('/api/policies/:policyNumber', { headers: { Authorization: Bearer ${token} } })
- Parse policy response and render PolicyCard.jsx components for each policy
- Add Button components for File a Claim (Link to /file-claim) and Download Proof (axios.get PDF endpoint with responseType: 'blob')
- Use react-router-dom Link for navigation, download PDF using file-saver library
- Add error handling for failed policy fetch with retry button

## Any Semantics
- Route path: /dashboard
- Protected route: Requires valid JWT token in localStorage
- Policy data source: GET /api/policies/:policyNumber
- PDF download endpoint: GET /api/policies/:policyNumber/proof
- Quick actions: File a Claim (navigation), Download Proof (file download)

## Json Output Shapes
- GET /api/policies/:policyNumber response: { policyNumber: 'POL123456', coverageType: 'Comprehensive', coverageLimit: 1000000, deductible: 500, effectiveDate: '2024-01-01', expiryDate: '2025-01-01' }
- GET /api/policies/:policyNumber/proof response: Blob (PDF file)

## List of Affected Files
- client/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx
- client/src/components/PolicyCard.jsx
- client/src/utils/api.js
- client/src/App.js (route configuration)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dashboard renders policy summary cards per mockup layout on successful sign-in
- Policy number, coverage type, limits, and deductibles display correctly from API response
- File a Claim button navigates to /file-claim route
- Download Proof of Insurance button downloads PDF from GET /api/policies/:policyNumber/proof endpoint
- Dashboard shows loading spinner while fetching policy data

## Test Plan
- Verify dashboard renders policy cards with correct data from API
- Verify File a Claim button navigates to /file-claim route
- Verify Download Proof of Insurance button downloads PDF file
- Verify loading spinner displays while policy data is being fetched
- Verify error message and retry button display if policy fetch fails

## Test Profile
Frontend data fetching, routing, and file download integration

## Technical Notes
- Use axios interceptors to attach Authorization header globally for authenticated requests
- Cache policy data in React Context or Redux to avoid redundant API calls
- Implement session timeout check before fetching policy data, redirect to /signin if token expired
- Use aria-live region for loading and error messages to support screen readers

## Related Stories
- Policy details backend API implementation
- Proof of insurance PDF generation service
- File a Claim FNOL submission flow
- My Profile personal information management screen

## Mockup Alignment
- Mockup shows policy summary cards with coverage details and two quick action buttons
- Use grid layout for policy cards, buttons styled per mockup with primary and secondary colors

title: Secure Sign In Screen with Email/Password and Optional MFA
body:
## Summary
Build the Secure Sign In screen with email/password form, client-side validation, MFA prompt on sign-in success, and password reset link.

## Current Behaviour
No sign-in screen exists for policyholders.

## Proposed Solution
Implement React sign-in page matching the Secure Sign In mockup, including form validation, session token storage, and MFA entry modal.

## Scope
- Create /signin route and SignIn component in client/src/pages/
- Add email and password input fields with real-time validation
- Integrate with POST /api/auth/signin backend endpoint
- Display MFA prompt modal if user has MFA enabled
- Add Forgot Password link routing to /reset-password

## Implementation
- Create SignIn.jsx component with form state using React useState
- Use react-router-dom v6 for navigation to /dashboard on success
- Call axios.post('/api/auth/signin', { email, password }) and handle 200/401/422 responses
- Store JWT token in localStorage as 'authToken' on successful sign-in
- Render MFAPrompt.jsx modal component if mfa_required flag is true
- Link Forgot Password to /reset-password route

## Any Semantics
- Route path: /signin
- Form method: POST to /api/auth/signin
- Success redirect: /dashboard
- Error handling: Display inline error messages below form fields
- Session token key: 'authToken' in localStorage

## Json Output Shapes
- POST /api/auth/signin response: { success: true, token: 'jwt_string', mfa_required: false, user: { id, email, fullName } }
- POST /api/auth/signin error: { success: false, error: 'Invalid credentials' }

## List of Affected Files
- client/src/pages/SignIn.jsx
- client/src/components/MFAPrompt.jsx
- client/src/utils/auth.js
- client/src/App.js (route configuration)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sign-in screen renders email and password fields per mockup layout
- Email validation enforces valid email format, password validation enforces minimum 8 characters
- Successful sign-in stores JWT token in localStorage and redirects to /dashboard
- MFA modal appears if backend response includes mfa_required: true flag
- Forgot Password link navigates to password reset flow

## Test Plan
- Verify sign-in screen renders all fields per mockup
- Verify email validation rejects invalid formats (missing @, etc.)
- Verify password validation rejects passwords shorter than 8 characters
- Verify successful sign-in with valid credentials stores token and redirects to /dashboard
- Verify MFA modal appears if mfa_required flag is returned
- Verify Forgot Password link navigates to /reset-password

## Test Profile
Frontend form validation, API integration testing, and navigation flow

## Technical Notes
- Use Formik or React Hook Form for form state management if complexity increases
- Implement WCAG AA keyboard navigation for form fields and buttons
- Add aria-label attributes to email and password inputs for screen reader support
- Consider adding reCAPTCHA to prevent automated login attempts

## Related Stories
- Password reset flow implementation
- MFA setup and verification backend endpoints
- Session timeout and token refresh mechanism
- Dashboard landing page after successful sign-in

## Mockup Alignment
- Mockup shows centered sign-in card with email/password fields, Sign In button, and Forgot Password link
- Use card layout with box-shadow and rounded corners per mockup styling

## Associated Mockups
- [projects/10817059263941828609/screens/6e6610e384ef4f58804300f07e7bf582](http://localhost:8000/wireframes/PRD-12/exports/view/3b83f488ab825ac9)
- [projects/10817059263941828609/screens/dbb1e885dabb4a5daa3cf1bc1edd903a](http://localhost:8000/wireframes/PRD-12/exports/view/53a946daf6a5aa74)
- [projects/10817059263941828609/screens/43901c5d01af4181a6005897aab5eb8b](http://localhost:8000/wireframes/PRD-12/exports/view/668f008e07ab47ba)

title: Repository Setup and Development Environment Configuration
body:
## Summary
Initialize the Canadian_Policyholder_Portal repository with React frontend, Express.js backend, ESLint/Prettier, CI pipeline, and environment variable scaffolding.

## Current Behaviour
No repository structure exists for the Canadian Policyholder Portal.

## Proposed Solution
Create monorepo structure with client/ and server/ directories, configure linting/formatting rules, add GitHub Actions for CI, and scaffold environment files for local development.

## Scope
- Create client/ folder with React app (CRA or Vite)
- Create server/ folder with Express.js boilerplate
- Configure ESLint, Prettier, and Husky pre-commit hooks
- Add .env.example files for client and server
- Configure GitHub Actions workflow for linting and build checks

## Implementation
- Initialize React app in client/ using create-react-app or Vite
- Initialize Express app in server/ with express-generator or manual setup
- Add ESLint config extending airbnb or standard, Prettier config with single quotes
- Add Husky and lint-staged for pre-commit linting
- Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with Node 18 matrix for lint and build steps
- Document local development setup in README.md

## Any Semantics
- Repository name: Canadian_Policyholder_Portal
- Client port: 3000 (default React dev server)
- Server port: 5000 (Express API)
- Environment variables: DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, AWS_S3_BUCKET, SESSION_SECRET

## Json Output Shapes
- JSON payload shapes should align to the backend contract for this workflow.

## List of Affected Files
- client/package.json
- client/src/index.js
- server/package.json
- server/app.js
- server/routes/index.js
- .eslintrc.json
- .prettierrc
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .env.example

## Acceptance Criteria
- Repository contains client/ and server/ directories with initial scaffolding
- npm install succeeds in both client/ and server/ directories
- ESLint and Prettier rules enforce code style on commit
- GitHub Actions workflow runs successfully on pull requests
- .env.example files document required environment variables

## Test Plan
- Verify npm install completes without errors in both client/ and server/
- Verify npm start launches React dev server on port 3000
- Verify npm start in server/ launches Express API on port 5000
- Verify ESLint and Prettier run on pre-commit hook
- Verify GitHub Actions CI workflow passes on sample pull request

## Test Profile
Local development and CI pipeline validation

## Technical Notes
- Use npm workspaces or Lerna if monorepo tooling is preferred
- Consider Vite for faster frontend builds compared to CRA
- Configure CORS in Express to allow client origin during local development
- Document Node version requirement (18.x or 20.x) in package.json engines field

## Related Stories
- Authentication backend API setup
- Frontend routing and layout structure
- Deployment pipeline configuration for staging and production
